title: narcissus scaberulus subsp. calcicola
intro: Narcissus scaberulus subsp. calcicola, also known as the calcicole scaber Narcissus, is a plant species within the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its unique adaptation to grow in areas with high calcium levels. The plant's bulb contains compounds that have been found to have medicinal properties, including potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The calcicole scaber Narcissus has been associated with several health benefits, including the ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions, although its use in modern medicine is still largely anecdotal and requires further scientific investigation.
